name: LoRA Fine-Tuning Pipeline
overview: Fine-tune a pretrained LLM on a custom dataset using LoRA adapters. Trains less than 1% of parameters, fits on consumer GPUs, and produces a mergeable adapter file. Use when prompt engineering is insufficient and you have labeled domain data.

steps.name: Dataset Preparation
steps.what: Format raw data into instruction-response pairs and split into train and validation sets.
steps.decision: 90/10 train/val split for datasets under 10K rows; 95/5 for larger. Format must match the model's chat template exactly — Llama 3 and Mistral use different delimiters. Verify with tokenizer.apply_chat_template before training.
failure_points: Chat template mismatch between training format and inference format — model sees a different special token structure at serving time and degrades.
Validation set contaminated with near-duplicates from training — use fuzzy dedup before splitting.

steps.name: Base Model Loading with Quantization
steps.what: Load the pretrained LLM in 4-bit or 8-bit precision to fit on limited GPU VRAM.
steps.decision: 4-bit nf4 for models above 7B on 24GB VRAM. 8-bit for models under 7B or when the task is numerically sensitive (nf4 degrades on regression-like outputs).
failure_points: dtype mismatch: base model loaded in bfloat16 but optimizer expects float32 — produces NaN loss in the first few steps.
bitsandbytes not installed for the correct CUDA version — raises ImportError at load time; match cuXX suffix to your driver.

steps.name: LoRA Configuration
steps.what: Define rank, alpha, target modules, and dropout for the low-rank adapter.
steps.decision: r=8, lora_alpha=16 for datasets under 10K samples. r=16-32 for datasets above 50K. Target q_proj and v_proj as baseline. Add k_proj and o_proj for reasoning-heavy tasks. Use lora_dropout=0.05 for small datasets.
failure_points: Rank too high for dataset size — adapter memorizes training examples and overfits without generalizing.
Wrong target_modules for the model architecture — each model (Llama, Mistral, Gemma) uses different projection names; check model.named_modules() to verify.

steps.name: PEFT Wrapping and Verification
steps.what: Wrap the quantized base model with LoRA adapters and verify trainable parameter count.
steps.decision: print_trainable_parameters must show under 1% of total params as trainable. If above 5%, target_modules is too broad. If 0%, the module names are wrong and no adapters were injected.
failure_points: Forgetting model.enable_input_require_grads() before get_peft_model — causes RuntimeError during backward pass on frozen embedding layers.
Trainable parameter count is 0 — target_modules names do not match the model's actual layer names.

steps.name: Training Setup and Execution
steps.what: Configure SFTTrainer with learning rate, batch size, gradient accumulation, and run the training loop.
steps.decision: batch_size=1 with gradient_accumulation_steps=4 to simulate batch of 4 on limited VRAM. Enable gradient_checkpointing for models above 7B. learning_rate=2e-4 is a safe starting point with cosine scheduler.
failure_points: Pad token not set — model attends to EOS as padding and learns corrupted attention patterns; always set tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token before training.
validation loss increasing while training loss decreases after epoch 2 on small datasets — add early stopping with patience=3.

steps.name: Adapter Saving and Optional Merge
steps.what: Save the LoRA adapter weights. Optionally merge them into the base model for deployment.
steps.decision: Save only the adapter (save_pretrained) for iterative experiments — adapter files are typically 10-100MB. Use merge_and_unload only for final production deployment to eliminate the runtime overhead of loading base + adapter separately.
failure_points: Merging while the model is still in 4-bit — merge_and_unload requires float16 or bfloat16; dequantize first with model.dequantize().
Loading a merged model without the original tokenizer — always save_pretrained both model and tokenizer to the same directory.

evaluation_checks: Trainable parameters < 1% of total model parameters after PEFT wrapping
Validation loss decreasing or stable through training — not diverging from training loss
Inference output format matches expected chat template on 10 held-out examples before full evaluation
common_failure_points: Rank too high for the dataset size, causing the adapter to memorize training examples and overfit without improving generalization.
Forgetting to enable input gradients with model.enable_input_require_grads() before wrapping with PEFT, causing a RuntimeError during backward pass on frozen embeddings.
dtype mismatch between the base model loaded in bfloat16 and the optimizer expecting float32, leading to NaN loss values within the first few training steps.
Out-of-memory errors because the model was not loaded in 4-bit, or because batch_size was increased without proportional reduction in sequence length.
Silent data corruption when the tokenizer pad token is not set, causing the model to attend to EOS tokens as padding and learn incorrect attention patterns.
